nameMeanings.name: Portman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (West Midlands): status name from Middle English portman ‘townsman, burgher, citizen’ (Old English portmann).

Dutch: from poort ‘gate’ + man ‘man’, an occupational name for a gatekeeper or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a walled town. In North America, this surname may also be an altered form of the more common variant Poortman.

variants.name: Sportsman
meanings: Possibly an altered form of Flemish Sportmans: patronymic from Poortman (see Portman 2), genitivized by the initial s (from des ‘of the’) and the suffix -s.

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Portman was born in Orchard Portman, Somerset, England as the son of Lord William Portman and Lady Elizabeth Gilbert. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Lady Joan Michell. He died on 15 January 1591, in Wiltshire, England, and was buried in Orchard Portman, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Portmanはイギリスソメルセトオルチャード ポルトマンでLord William PortmanとLady Elizabeth Gilbertの息子として生まれた。彼はLady Joan Michellとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1591年1月15日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアで死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトオルチャード ポルトマンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Portman(은)는 영국 서머세트 오처드 포트먼에서 Lord William Portman(와)과 Lady Elizabeth Gilbert의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Lady Joan Michell(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1591년 1월 15일 영국 윌츠히르에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 오처드 포트먼에 묻혔다.

У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Lady Joan Michell.
